origin.name: Ukraine
origin.death_registration: DRACS (State Registry of Acts of Civil Status)
origin.post_mortem_trigger: Violent, suspicious, unexplained, or conflict-related deaths
origin.key_document: Death certificate from DRACS (requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Ukraine travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Kyiv guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Ukraine is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option. The ongoing conflict may complicate arrangements in affected regions.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from DRACS (certified English translation required)
Prosecutor's clearance (for unnatural or conflict-related deaths)
special_rules: The FCDO advises against all travel to Ukraine due to the ongoing conflict. Consular assistance in conflict-affected areas is severely limited.
The current conflict may significantly extend timelines, particularly in areas of active hostilities.
All Ukrainian-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Contact the FCDO emergency line immediately: +44 (0)20 7008 5000.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Ukrainian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Kyiv provides limited consular services; contact the FCDO emergency line first. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Ukraine to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Ukraine. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-3 weeks in normal circumstances. DRACS documentation, translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
